heading: 2.1.3 What’s the big picture?
text:
book tools, skills, knowledge taught come 
growing awareness demand [open science][open-science],
[reproducible research][reproducibility], 
[software sustainability][sustainable-software]. ?[Open science][open-science] movement make scientific data, methods,
results publicly freely accessible publishing 
[open licenses][open-license]. licenses allow others re-use modify
outputs without worrying copyright infringement, often requiring
attribution original source.[Reproducible research][reproducible-research] means ensuring anyone 
access data software can feasibly reproduce similar results,
check build . ’s important note science can
open reproducible reproducible open.[Sustainable software][sustainable-software] software developed 
way makes easier people extend /maintain , rather
replace . Sustainability isn’t just software , also
depends skills culture users. instance, software
package maintained couple post-docs paid 
fraction earn industry realistic hope 
promotion field doesn’t value tool building, doesn’t matter
whether package well tested easy install: sooner later
(probably sooner) become [abandonware][abandonware].book presents software development emphasizes openness,
reproducibility, sustainability, supports individual
researchers entire research community.

heading: 2.2 Introduction for instructors
text:
instructional design learning material contained 
book revolves around three key concepts:Participatory live-coding lessons, participants join 
instructors write troubleshoot code step--step. believe
method encourages participants actively engage 
material, build muscle memory typing, learn 
handle mistakes, rather passively observing content.
(Brown Wilson, 2018; Wilson, 2019a)-class --class exercises include independent
reading assigned material hands-, practical work.
hands-exercises interspersed throughout live-coding
sessions complement reinforce content well
give learners opportunity work code problems
pace. Reading activities used build concept-heavy
knowledge.Project- problem-based assignments include independent
group-based work. project assignments, learners reinforce
learned new problem challenge. independent
work prepares optional group-based work, 
can practice team-based skills like communication, project management,
running meetings.details instructors can found Appendix TODO: Add ref label.
